habitat: Lowland forests near streams/ponds; gardens; parks; wooded areas; and mangroves
story: Few birds capture the imagination quite like the Black Backed Dwarf Kingfisher (Ceyx erithaca). As a member of the Alcedinidae family, it has carved out a unique niche in Lowland forests near streams/ponds; gardens; parks; wooded areas; and mangroves. Its conservation status is Near Threatened (NT), a classification that drives crucial research and protection efforts. The Black Backed Dwarf Kingfisher's presence is a vital sign of its ecosystem's health, making its preservation a priority. This poster is a tribute to the Black Backed Dwarf Kingfisher, celebrating its role in the natural world and inspiring a deeper appreciation for biodiversity.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
